title: Code Review Findings
description: Structured output schema for code review sub-agents

reviewer.description: Persona name that produced this output (e.g., 'correctness', 'security')

findings.description: List of code review findings. Empty array if no issues found.

title.description: Short, specific issue title. 10 words or fewer.

file.description: Relative file path from repository root

line.description: Primary line number of the issue

why_it_matters.description: Impact and failure mode -- not 'what is wrong' but 'what breaks'

autofix_class.description: Reviewer's conservative recommendation for how this issue should be handled after synthesis

owner.description: Who should own the next action for this finding after synthesis

requires_verification.description: Whether any fix for this finding must be re-verified with targeted tests or a follow-up review pass

suggested_fix.description: Concrete minimal fix. Omit or null if no good fix is obvious -- a bad suggestion is worse than none.

confidence.description: Reviewer confidence in this finding, as one of 5 discrete anchors with behavioral definitions (see _meta.confidence_anchors). Pick the anchor whose definition matches; do not interpolate.

evidence.description: Code-grounded evidence: snippets, line references, or pattern descriptions. At least 1 item.

pre_existing.description: True if this issue exists in unchanged code unrelated to the current diff

residual_risks.description: Risks the reviewer noticed but could not confirm as findings

testing_gaps.description: Missing test coverage the reviewer identified

confidence_anchors.0: Not a real finding / false positive. Do not report.
confidence_anchors.25: Speculative. Weak or circumstantial evidence; likely noise. Do not report.
confidence_anchors.50: Plausible but unverified -- real-but-uncertain. Do not report unless P0, or unless cross-reviewer corroboration promotes it to 75 during merge.
confidence_anchors.75: Confident. Real and substantiated with concrete, code-grounded evidence. Report.
confidence_anchors.100: Certain. Verifiable from the code/diff alone -- a definitive logic bug, a compile/type error, or a quotable standards violation with no interpretation. Report.

_meta.confidence_gate: Suppress below anchor 75. Exception: P0 at anchor 50+ survives. The gate is applied after dedup and cross-reviewer promotion, so an anchor-50 finding corroborated by a second reviewer (promoted to 75) survives.

severity_definitions.P0: Critical breakage, exploitable vulnerability, data loss/corruption. Must fix before merge.
severity_definitions.P1: High-impact defect likely hit in normal usage, breaking contract. Should fix.
severity_definitions.P2: Moderate issue with meaningful downside (edge case, perf regression, maintainability trap). Fix if straightforward.
severity_definitions.P3: Low-impact, narrow scope, minor improvement. User's discretion.

autofix_classes.auto_apply: Local, deterministic code or test fix safe to apply automatically -- correctness, error handling, security, performance, mechanical code quality, with no question about the author's intent. Examples: extract duplicated helper, add missing nil check, fix off-by-one, add missing test, remove dead code. Do not default to fyi when a concrete safe fix exists.
autofix_classes.ask_user: A concrete issue the user should review because it changes behavior, permissions, contracts, product decisions, or otherwise touches the author's deliberate intent -- OR it is actionable work that needs a design decision or cross-cutting change. Presented to the user; never auto-applied. Examples: add auth to an unprotected endpoint, change an API response shape, redesign a data model, choose between architectural approaches.
autofix_classes.fyi: Informational or operational item surfaced in the report only -- no action required. Examples: design asymmetry the PR improves but does not fully resolve, residual risk notes, deployment considerations, acknowledged tradeoffs.

owners.review-fixer: The in-skill fixer applies this automatically in mutating modes (auto_apply only).
owners.human: A person reviews and decides. All ask_user findings, plus informational items left to the reader.
owners.release: Operational or rollout follow-up; do not convert into code-fix work automatically.

return_tiers.description: Finding fields are split into two tiers. The full schema (with all required fields) applies to the artifact file on disk. The compact return to the orchestrator omits detail-tier fields. Both are valid uses of this schema in different contexts.
return_tiers.merge_tier: Returned to orchestrator: title, severity, file, line, confidence, autofix_class, owner, requires_verification, pre_existing, suggested_fix (optional). Plus top-level reviewer, residual_risks, testing_gaps.
return_tiers.detail_tier: Required in artifact file, omitted from compact return: why_it_matters, evidence. The artifact file must pass full schema validation including all required fields. Headless output depends on why_it_matters and evidence being present in the artifact.
